verification.note: Section 195 requires TDS at 30% on NRO interest and Section 115A sets a flat 20% rate on dividends paid to a non-resident (other than Section 115-O company dividends) - both well-established, long-standing rates. The capital-gains TDS rates mirror the domestic Sections 111A/112A rates, since Indian brokers/AMCs withhold at the same rate that applies to the final tax. Cross-checked against the Income Tax Department's own non-resident taxation guide and multiple current NRI filing guides; re-check only if a Budget/Finance Act amendment is reported.

source_refs: Income Tax Act, 1961, Section 195 (TDS on payments to a non-resident)
Income Tax Act, 1961, Section 115A (tax on dividends, interest, royalties, fees for a non-resident)
rules/nri-dtaa.json (per-country treaty rates, applied when more beneficial than these domestic defaults)

values.note: These are the domestic default rates before any DTAA relief - a Tax Residency Certificate and Form 10F let the payer withhold at a lower treaty rate instead, where rules/nri-dtaa.json's nro_withholding_rates records one for the country. Section 115A dividend tax is always the lower of this 20% domestic rate and the treaty rate, never the higher one, even if the treaty rate happens to exceed 20%. Surcharge and health-and-education cess are not included in any rate here.
